overview: A documentary chronicling photographer Julius Shulman, whose images defined Southern California's mid-century modern architecture.
title: Visual Acoustics: The Modernism of Julius Shulman

overview: A portrait of husband-and-wife design duo Charles and Ray Eames and their influence on mid-century modern furniture and architecture.
title: Eames: The Architect and the Painter

overview: Architect Nathaniel Kahn explores the life and modernist buildings of his father, Louis Kahn.
title: My Architect: A Son's Journey

overview: Sydney Pollack profiles longtime friend Frank Gehry, examining the architect's design process and body of work.
title: Sketches of Frank Gehry

overview: A playful look inside Rem Koolhaas's Maison a Bordeaux, narrated by the housekeeper who has cared for it since it was built.
title: Koolhaas Houselife

overview: A portrait of architect Norman Foster, tracing his career from student sketches to landmark structures like the Reichstag dome and 30 St Mary Axe.
title: How Much Does Your Building Weigh, Mr Foster?

overview: A filmmaker investigates the fate of Luis Barragan's professional archive after it was acquired as a corporate gift and relocated to Switzerland.
title: The Proposal

overview: A visiting Korean book translator and a young architecture enthusiast form a bond amid the modernist buildings of Columbus, Indiana.
title: Columbus

overview: Danish urban researcher Jan Gehl's decades of work reshaping cities around pedestrians rather than cars.
title: The Human Scale

overview: A meditative survey of Antoni Gaudi's buildings in and around Barcelona, from Casa Batllo to the unfinished Sagrada Familia.
title: Antonio Gaudi

overview: A two-part portrait of the architect's turbulent life and his reshaping of American residential and civic design.
title: Frank Lloyd Wright

overview: The rise and demolition of the Pruitt-Igoe housing project in St. Louis, and what its failure reveals about postwar urban planning.
title: The Pruitt-Igoe Myth

overview: A survey of the design decisions, planners, and architects shaping cities around the world.
title: Urbanized

overview: An examination of industrial design and the people who shape the everyday objects we use.
title: Objectified

overview: A portrait of architect Eero Saarinen, from the TWA Flight Center to the Gateway Arch, narrated with home movies shot by his son.
title: Eero Saarinen: The Architect Who Saw the Future
